2604.08424.title: On-board Telemetry Monitoring in Autonomous Satellites: Challenges and Opportunities
2604.08424.paper_summary: The increasing autonomy of spacecraft demands fault-detection systems that are both reliable and explainable. This work addresses eXplainable Artificial Intelligence for onboard Fault Detection, Isolation and Recovery within the Attitude and Orbit Control Subsystem by introducing a framework that enhances interpretability in neural anomaly detectors. We propose a method to derive low-dimensional, semantically annotated encodings from intermediate neural activations, called peepholes. Applied to a convolutional autoencoder, the framework produces interpretable indicators that enable the identification and localization of anomalies in reaction-wheel telemetry. Peepholes analysis further reveals bias detection and supports fault localization. The proposed framework enables the semantic characterization of detected anomalies while requiring only a marginal increase in computational resources, thus supporting its feasibility for on-board deployment.
2604.08424.paper_summary_zh: 太空船日益增強的自主性要求故障檢測系統既可靠又可解釋。這項工作針對航向和軌道控制子系統中的可解釋人工智慧，提出了一個增強神經異常檢測器可解釋性的框架。我們提出了一種從中間神經激活中推導低維度、語義標註編碼的方法，稱為“窺視孔”。應用於卷積自編碼器，該框架生成可解釋的指標，使得能夠識別和定位反應輪遙測中的異常。窺視孔分析進一步揭示了偏差檢測並支持故障定位。所提出的框架使檢測到的異常能夠進行語義特徵描述，同時僅需微小增加計算資源，從而支持其在機載部署中的可行性。
2604.08424.author: Lorenzo Capelli et.al.
2604.08424.authors: Lorenzo Capelli, Leandro de Souza Rosa, Maurizio De Tommasi, Livia Manovi, Andriy Enttsel, Mauro Mangia, Riccardo Rovatti, Ilaria Pinci, Carlo Ciancarelli, Eleonora Mariotti, Gianluca Furano
